greeting: Morning, Haniel. Friday, July 4th Eve — clean calendar, five focused blocks. Let's close this week strong.
hype: You cranked out the recurrence engine, Stripe integration, rollover logic, and the full battle-test matrix while most people were sleeping. Phase 2 is one PDF pipeline and one Chatwoot channel away from done. Today you close it, plant the AZ brand, kick Estimates into motion, and cap the afternoon with assemblies on the Takeoff Engine. This is the session that makes the whole week count.
notice: July 4th weekend — plan wraps at 17:15, leaving you a clean close before the long weekend.

schedule.title: Brand: indigo/slate palette + logo assets + Company Profile settings section
PDF pipeline (DomPDF invoice template + AZ letterhead, shared Money)
Module A — Estimates (+ line-item builder, accept→version/ad-hoc, PDF)

tasks.title: Brand: indigo/slate palette + logo assets + Company Profile settings section
tasks.notes: Brand identity trailing into the settings build — t7 feeds the PDF letterhead in t19

prep.what: Set AZ Team AI's visual identity: implement the indigo/slate Tailwind palette, wire logo assets, and build the Company Profile settings section.
prep.understand: This is a Module-project fork, so the branding settings layer (`SettingsManager`, zero-hardcode) is already in place (Module t10/t28/t29 pattern). The palette is indigo for primary and slate for neutral — both are native Tailwind scales. Company Profile fields (name, address, phone, email, logo URL) will feed t19's PDF letterhead and every client-facing screen. Do this first so t19 has real data to pull from.
approach: Extend `tailwind.config.js` — remap the `primary` scale to indigo (primary-50..primary-900) and `neutral` to slate; verify the sidebar and login button pick up the new color immediately.

Add AZ logo assets under `public/images/` (logo.svg + logo-dark.svg), then add `logo_path` / `logo_dark_path` setting keys in `SettingsSeeder` and expose them through `HandleInertiaRequests` in the shared Inertia props.

Create a Company Profile settings section: a new Blade/Inertia settings page with fields (`app_name`, `company_address`, `company_phone`, `company_email`) persisted via `SettingsManager::setMany()`. Reuse the existing branding settings form pattern from Module.
Smoke: hit the login page and dashboard — confirm indigo primary color, AZ logo in nav/login panel, and Company Profile fields save and round-trip correctly.
resources: Tailwind color customization docs — tailwindcss.com/docs/customizing-colors
Module-project t10 (branding settings CRUD) and t28/t29 (login rebuild + settings hardening) for the exact patterns to follow
`SettingsManager::setMany()` — already implemented in Module; use it for the Company Profile form submit
AZ Team AI logo files (indigo mark) — grab from wherever they live on your machine

tasks.title: PDF pipeline (DomPDF invoice template + AZ letterhead, shared Money)
tasks.notes: Last remaining Phase 2 item alongside t20; unblocks invoice sending and the client portal

prep.what: Build the invoice PDF generator using DomPDF: a Blade template with AZ letterhead that pulls from Company Profile settings and uses the shared Money VO for cent-correct dollar formatting.
prep.understand: DomPDF is already installed (it ships with Module). The billing data model is fully built — Invoice, InvoiceLineItem, totals service, doc numbering, all done in t12–t24. The `Money` value object from the totals service formats integer cents as "$1,234.56" so you never do division in the template. Letterhead data (logo, company name, address) comes from the Company Profile settings you wired in t7 — do t7 first. This PDF route is the output channel for invoice emails and the client portal.
approach: Create `resources/views/pdf/invoice.blade.php`: AZ letterhead block (logo img from `setting('logo_path')` + company name/address), invoice header (INV number, issue date, due date, client name+address from `$invoice->subscription->project->client`), line-items table (description, qty, unit price, total via `Money::format()`), totals block (subtotal, tax, total due), payment instructions footer.
Build `App\Services\InvoicePdfService`: takes an `Invoice` model, merges the data bag, calls `Pdf::loadView('pdf.invoice', $data)->setPaper('a4')->stream()` or `->download()`.
Add `GET /invoices/{invoice}/pdf` to `routes/web.php`, gated by `can:view,invoice`; controller action calls `InvoicePdfService::stream($invoice)`.

Smoke test: `artisan tinker` — grab the first seeded Invoice, call `InvoicePdfService::stream()` and eyeball the output. Watch for DomPDF memory errors on large line-item sets (bump memory limit in `config/dompdf.php` if needed).
resources: `barryvdh/laravel-dompdf` — already in composer.json; README for `Pdf` facade usage and config
Module-project t33 (PDF pipeline pattern) if it has been implemented there already — copy the pattern
`Money` value object from the billing totals service (t16 — `app/ValueObjects/Money.php` or similar path)
`setting('logo_path')`, `setting('company_name')` etc. — from t7 Company Profile settings

tasks.title: NotificationChannel abstraction + ChatwootChannel (SMS, live-tested)
tasks.notes: Closes Phase 2 alongside t19; the reminders dispatcher (t22, done) needs this channel to fire

prep.what: Build a `NotificationChannel` interface and a `ChatwootChannel` implementation that fires SMS-style messages to clients via the Chatwoot REST API, then live-test against a real contact.
prep.understand: Chatwoot is AZ Team AI's client messaging hub. The reminders dispatcher (t22, done) and reconciliation digest already exist and need a channel to fire through. The `chatwoot_contact_links` table (created in t14 migration) maps each client to their Chatwoot contact ID. The Chatwoot API flow is: look up the contact ID → find or create a conversation → POST a message. The abstraction (`NotificationChannel` interface) keeps the dispatcher decoupled so you can add email later without touching the dispatcher.
approach: Define `App\Contracts\NotificationChannel` with a single `send(mixed $recipient, string $message): void` method.

Implement `App\Channels\ChatwootChannel`: resolve the recipient's `chatwoot_contact_links` row, call `GET {CHATWOOT_URL}/api/v1/accounts/{id}/contacts/{contact_id}/conversations` to find an open conversation (or create one via POST), then `POST /conversations/{conv_id}/messages` with `{"message_type": "outgoing", "content": $message}` using Bearer token from `CHATWOOT_API_TOKEN` env var.
Bind `NotificationChannel::class` to `ChatwootChannel::class` in `AppServiceProvider` (or a dedicated `ChannelServiceProvider`) so the reminders dispatcher gets it via DI.

Live-test: fire `artisan reminders:dispatch` (or trigger via tinker) with a real pending invoice linked to a contact in `chatwoot_contact_links` — confirm the message lands in your Chatwoot inbox within seconds.
resources: Chatwoot REST API docs — `{your-chatwoot-domain}/swagger` or docs.chatwoot.com/docs/apis/rest (conversations + messages endpoints)
`chatwoot_contact_links` table — created in t14 migration; has `client_id` → `chatwoot_contact_id` mapping
Reminders dispatcher — `app/Services/RemindersDispatcher.php` from t22; inject `NotificationChannel` into its constructor
`CHATWOOT_URL` + `CHATWOOT_API_TOKEN` + `CHATWOOT_ACCOUNT_ID` — add to `.env` if not already there

tasks.title: Module A — Estimates (+ line-item builder, accept→version/ad-hoc, PDF)
tasks.notes: Shared foundations from t41 are done; t42 assembles them into a working estimate flow

prep.what: Build the Estimates backend module A: line-item builder with totals, acceptance flow that triggers a ProjectVersion freeze or an ad-hoc invoice, and PDF generation via the t19 pipeline.
prep.understand: The shared foundations from t41 are fully done: `DocumentTotalsCalculator`, `VersionFactory`, `VersionApprovalService`, `cancelStripeThenVoid`, `ClientCreditLedger`, and document number seeds. The `Estimate` model was scaffolded by `make:module` (t13). The key branch on acceptance: if the project has a recurring `ProjectVersion` plan → `VersionApprovalService` freezes a new version; if it's ad-hoc → `InvoiceFactory` mints a single invoice directly. The PDF reuses `InvoicePdfService` from t19 with a separate estimate Blade template.
approach: Flesh out `EstimateLineItem` (description, quantity, unit_price in cents, tax_rate) and wire `DocumentTotalsCalculator::calculate($lineItems)` to compute subtotal/tax/total. Store results on `Estimate` (`subtotal_cents`, `tax_cents`, `total_cents`).

Implement `EstimateController::approve(Estimate $estimate)`: validate state is `draft`|`sent`, call `VersionApprovalService::approveFromEstimate($estimate)` which branches — recurring project → `VersionFactory::fromEstimate()`, ad-hoc → `InvoiceFactory::adHocFromEstimate()`.

Build `resources/views/pdf/estimate.blade.php` (same letterhead structure as invoice.blade.php but with "ESTIMATE" header + EST- doc number) and add `Estimate` handling to `InvoicePdfService` (or a sibling `EstimatePdfService`).

Wire routes: `POST /estimates`, `GET /estimates/{id}`, `POST /estimates/{id}/approve`, `GET /estimates/{id}/pdf`. Smoke: create an estimate via tinker, approve it, verify a Version or Invoice was created.
resources: `VersionApprovalService`, `VersionFactory`, `DocumentTotalsCalculator` — all from t41 shared foundations (already in `app/Services/Billing/`)
`InvoicePdfService` from t19 (build t19 first today before tackling this)
Estimate model scaffolded by `make:module Estimate` in t13 — look at the generated controller/policy/requests as the starting scaffold
Document number sequences seeded in t41 — look for the `EST-` prefix in the doc-number seeds

tasks.title: Assemblies-per-room → paint/drywall/flooring quantities + wall heights
tasks.notes: assemblies-lite scaffold already in engine/assemblies.py; full version adds classification registry + wall height dimension

prep.what: Complete the assemblies engine: given a classified room with area + perimeter, compute paint SF, flooring SF, drywall SF, and ceiling SF, and emit them as CSI-coded line items in the Excel assemblies tab.
prep.understand: `engine/assemblies.py` has the lite scaffold from the B1 service shell (t6). The full version needs a classification-to-assembly registry — e.g., "Living Room" → [flooring (area SF), wall paint (perimeter × height SF), ceiling paint (area SF)]. The critical missing dimension is wall height: a 2D floor plan gives area and perimeter but no height. The `wall_height_ft` PATCH route already exists on the service (from t28 Phase A), so the Sheet model can carry it via a migration. The existing `build_sheet_takeoff` orchestrator in `service/worker.py` is the hook. Gauntlet baseline is 85/85 — don't break it.
approach: Define the assembly template registry in `engine/assemblies.py`: a dict `ASSEMBLY_TEMPLATES = {classification: [(csi_code, description, formula_fn), ...]}` where `formula_fn(area, perimeter, height_ft)` returns a quantity. Cover: Living Room, Bedroom, Bathroom, Kitchen, Hallway, plus a `_default` fallback. Start with flooring (area), wall paint (perimeter×height), ceiling paint (area) for most; add tile walls for Bathroom.
Add `wall_height_ft: float = 9.0` to the `Sheet` Alembic migration (nullable with default) and expose it in the Sheet Pydantic schema. Pass it from the worker down to `assemble_room()` calls.

Implement `assemble_room(classification, area, perimeter, height_ft) -> list[AssemblyResult]` in `engine/assemblies.py` where `AssemblyResult` is a namedtuple of `(csi_code, description, quantity, unit)`. Run the registry lookup, apply formulas, return the list.

Hook into `build_sheet_takeoff` in `service/worker.py`: after rooms are measured, call `assemble_room()` per room and write results to Excel Sheet 5 via `engine/excel.py`. Run `python -m pytest gt/` to confirm gauntlet stays 85/85.
resources: `engine/assemblies.py` — existing lite scaffold; start here, extend don't rewrite
`engine/excel.py` — 5-tab workbook; Sheet 5 is the assemblies output tab
`service/worker.py` — `build_sheet_takeoff` orchestrator, the integration hook for assemblies
CSI MasterFormat division 09 for finishes: 09 91 00 = painting, 09 60 00 = flooring, 09 29 00 = gypsum board/drywall
`wall_height_ft` PATCH route in `service/routes.py` — from t28 Phase A; the model field you need to add a migration for
